the_signal.headline: MCP server security moves from afterthought to production requirement
the_signal.body: n8n's MCP security guide reveals that tool-call scoping, auth, and observability aren't optional—they're the difference between a working agent and a compromised revenue system. As agents move into production workflows, security gaps become liability vectors that directly impact GTM credibility and customer trust.

tool_drop.name: Safari MCP Server
tool_drop.what: WebKit's native MCP implementation that lets agents interact with Safari directly via standardized protocol.
tool_drop.why: Signals that browser automation is moving from janky scraping to protocol-native agent integrations—your agent stacks should start planning for this parity.

thread_of_day.title: The case against shipping half-baked AI products
thread_of_day.takeaway: Rushing to production without validating agent behavior under review workflows erodes the trust that enterprise GTM buyers need to bet on AI systems.

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills with cognee and n8n

whats_moving.context: n8n's comparison of 10+ options signals that RAG pipeline cost and latency are now deal-breakers for enterprise deployments—your architecture choice directly impacts margin.
5-level benchmark frameworks are becoming table stakes for justifying AI spend to execs—GTM teams need to speak this language when pitching AI-powered revenue systems internally.

whats_moving.topic: Geopolitical AI risk (Claude Code bans at Alibaba)
whats_moving.context: Enterprise security teams are now treating AI tool access as compliance risk, not convenience—expect more tooling bans and vendor scrutiny in regulated markets.

act_on_this: Audit your production agent workflows for MCP server security gaps—specifically tool-call scoping and observability—before your first enterprise customer asks about it in a security review.

numbers.stat: 14× faster embeddings in Manticore's ONNX rebuild
numbers.context: Embedding latency directly impacts agent response times; this kind of infrastructure optimization is becoming table stakes for competitive vector retrieval in production systems.
